items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, marking significant investment in San José's commercial corridor. This is the largest project in this permit batch and could bring new businesses or industrial activity to the area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering an amnesty program to help businesses in shopping centers come into compliance with licensing requirements. This could affect local business operations and tax compliance across the city's retail centers.

items.headline: City announces summer 'Shop Local' campaign and new retail incentives
items.summary: Cupertino is launching a Summer of Celebrations campaign to promote local shopping and introducing economic development incentives targeting retail, small businesses, and startups. The program includes public-private partnerships for city event sponsorships.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pickup designated annual city-sponsored event
items.summary: City Council approved making the Great American Litter Pickup an official annual city-sponsored event and authorized spending city funds on it, plus accepting donated materials and services. This gives the popular cleanup initiative official city backing and resources.

items.headline: Federal Housing Grants Available for Local Projects
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission will review Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications on March 25. These federal grants support affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study San Antonio Road transportation and land use changes
items.summary: The Commission will examine alternatives for how San Antonio Road should develop, including transportation options and potential land use changes. This could affect traffic patterns, development opportunities, and neighborhood character in that corridor.

items.headline: New retail vitality policies coming to zoning code
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending its zoning and building regulations to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. This could change requirements for new commercial development and potentially affect how retail spaces are designed or regulated citywide.

items.headline: Los Altos adopts new Below Market Rate housing exemption policy
items.summary: The City Council approved a resolution allowing certain Below Market Rate (BMR) units to transfer ownership while maintaining affordability restrictions. This policy change could affect how affordable housing units are managed and transferred in Los Altos.
